This is how they feel about the problems that they have. You just want to expand that. And it's just an exercise, really. I mean, doing the avatar is an imaginative exercise. And of course, it's not the same as the real people out there. But it's a way that you can focus on who that person is that you're, you're trying to reach. And now, if you don't like this by any, you know, if you don't like kind of creating a composite of different traits and imagining a person, then the next thing to do is very simple is just think of someone that you know, or that you've experienced, or a customer, or someone like that, who you've dealt with, who is pretty much like the types of people that you, you feel like, okay, this, this is going to be my avatar. They're just going to represent it. So at least the point is that you want to get a feeling for the specific person you're talking to. And you want to feel, because then when you're developing a meaningful relationship, a meaningful conversation with them, you want to speak directly to that person, like, hey, you know, this is, this is something that I think will really help you, or this is how you thought about the things this way.
